titlestmt.titleproper: Finding Aid for the Eugene Rodney Papers PASC.0163
titlestmt.author: Finding aid prepared by UCLA Performing Arts Special Collections Staff, pre-1999; machine-readable finding aid created by Caroline Cubé after initial encoding by Julie Graham.

p.date: Online finding aid last updated 2026 April.

address.addressline: Room A1713, Charles E. Young Research Library

publicationstmt.p: Finding aid description and metadata in this record are licensed under a Creative Commons Public Domain Designation (CC0 1.0 Universal). See LSC's Metadata Rights Statement for more information.

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2026-04-27 13:57:50 -0700.

did.abstract: Eugene Rodney was best known as producer of the television program Father Knows Best. The bulk of the collection includes scripts and production information for the radio and television series Father Knows Best and the short lived television series Window on Main Street, featuring Robert Young.
did.physloc: Stored off-site. All requests to access special collections material must be made in advance using the request button located on this page.

accessrestrict.head: Restrictions on Access
accessrestrict.p: Portions of collection unprocessed. Material in the Accession LSC-1986-040 backlog series is unavailable for access. Please see LSC's Access to Unprocessed Collections Policy for more information about levels of discovery and access of special collections materials and registering interest in unprocessed collections.

phystech.head: Physical Characteristics and Technical Requirements
phystech.p: CONTAINS UNPROCESSED AUDIOVISUAL MATERIALS: Audiovisual materials are not currently available for access and will require further processing and assessment. If you have questions about this material please email AskLSC@library.ucla.edu.

userestrict.head: Restrictions on Use and Reproduction
userestrict.p: Property rights to the physical objects belong to UCLA Library Special Collections. All other rights, including copyright, are retained by the creators and their heirs. It is the responsibility of the researcher to determine who holds the copyright and pursue the copyright owner or his or her heir for permission to publish where The UC Regents do not hold the copyright.

odd.head: UCLA Catalog Record ID
odd.p: UCLA Catalog Record ID: 9927231373606533

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: [Identification of item], Eugene Rodney papers (Collection PASC 163). UCLA Library Special Collections, Charles E. Young Research Library, University of California, Los Angeles.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Processed by UCLA Performing Arts Special Collections Staff, pre-1999.

Collections are processed to a variety of levels depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived user interest and research value, availability of staff and resources, and competing priorities. Library Special Collections provides a standard level of preservation and access for all collections and, when time and resources permit, conducts more intensive processing. These materials have been arranged and described according to national and local standards and best practices.

We are committed to providing ethical, inclusive, and anti-racist description of the materials we steward, and to remediating existing description of our materials that contains language that may be offensive or cause harm. We invite you to submit feedback about how our collections are described, and how they could be described more accurately, by filling out the form located on our website: Report Problematic Content and Description in UCLA's library collections and archives.

bioghist.head: Biography
bioghist.p: Eugene B. Rodney was originally a publicist for East Coast theater owners and stage producers. He moved west when a theater chain he was managing was sold. In Hollywood, he became an associate producer at Reliance Pictures and was involved in the filming of The Last of the Mohicans, The Count of Monte Cristo and Red Salute with Robert Young. Rodney went on to become the Vice-President of Production for Robert Young's production company, Cavalier Productions, Inc. and helped conceptualize and then produce the television version of Father Knows Best. Rodney died in November, 1985.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: The collection consists of materials related to the career of television producer and director Eugene Rodney. The bulk of the collection comprises files for the radio and television versions of Father Knows Best and short lived television series, Window On Main Street. Father Knows Best radio scripts represent 1949-1954; the television files represent ca. 1955-ca. 1960 and include scripts and production material. Window on Main Street scripts and production information represent thirty-plus episodes. Additionally, there is a very small amount of Rodney's personal business files, a small number of photographs of Father Knows Best cast members, and a small number of screenplays for projects in which Rodney was involved in some capacity. Additional materials consist of vinyl records that contain recordings of music from Father Knows Best.

arrangement.head: Organization and Arrangement
arrangement.p:
Arranged in the following series:
Radio Projects
Telelvision Projects
Personal Business Papers
Unidentified Projects

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: The Radio Project series documents Rodney's involvement with the radio version of Father Knows Best, which started as a radio sitcom in 1949 on NBC.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: Television projects files mainly represent the television series Father Knows Best . The files may contain scripts in various drafts, some annotated, basic production information such as opening credits, contracts, daily reports, wardrobe requirements, and cast and crew sheets. Additionally there are files related to Window on Main Street and a very small number of other projects. The series is arranged alphabetically by television series and episode title.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Assorted undated outlines, drafts, treatments, and other material regarding story ideas for half-hour television shows.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: First and second drafts by Dorothy Cooper, dated January 3, 1960 and January 12, 1960. Possibly for new television series.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Set of two polished marble and brass placques awarded to Father Knows Best for Best Situation Comedy Series from 1958 to 1959.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Three certificates awarded to Father Knows Best from 1958-1959 as Best Situation Comedy Series. Robert Young and Jane Wyatt are also noted as Outstanding Actor and Actress. Signed by the director.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Various contracts, agreements, financial statements, screenwriter correspondence, company information, and other material.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Income schedules and budget statements from Bisgeier, Breslauer & Co. for Eugene Rodney.

odd.p: Cast biographies, budget and contract information, musical cue sheets, Writers Guild Reports, legal presentation instructions, and other material.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Accounting contracts from Bisgeier, Breslauer & Co., budget lists, information on Temopic Enterprises, and other material.

did.unittitle: WINDOW ON MAIN STREET title cards

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Set of two black and white title cards for the television series.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: Included are Father Knows Best cast member stills, insurance company information, business and personal correspondence, and a small amount of correspondence and clippings regarding the Myasthenia Gravis Foundation. There is no specific arrangement to the series.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Box contains 42 various Father Knows Best cast member stills, insurance company information, Robert Young file, correspondence and other material.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Box contains Rodney Bel Air Property information, personal correspondence and business matters, lear jet contracts, Myasthenia Gravis Foundation correspondence, and other material.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Box contains publishing correspondence, legal matters, insurance contracts, Goldstone agreements, and other miscellaneous folders.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Correspondence and clippings, etc. from Robert Young regarding the Myasthenia Gravis Foundation.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: The series includes files which are not identified with known projects and in which Rodney was involved in some capacity, and which may or may not have been produced. Mostly includes drafts of screenplays with occasional production information for projects such as The Fan Letter , The Happy Scoundrel , and 1600 Pennsylvania Avenue , among others. The series is arranged alphabetically by project title.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Screenplay by Berne Geler (based on story by Sidney Biddell and Frederic Frank), dated October 29, 1951. Includes various drafts and re-writes.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Various scripts and articles, including revised first draft step-outlines, treatments with notes, and artist renditions the White House used as research for the screenplay.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Screenplay by Sidney Biddell and Fredric Frank, dated November 1951. With various drafts, basic material and re-writes.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Incomplete draft revision, dated December 4, 1951.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Producer's Copy of screenplay by Roswell Rogers, no date.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Various letters, handwritten notes, and story outlines for screenplay to star Robert Young.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Production information: shooting schedule, story outlines, stills used for scene research, and other material.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Several screenplay drafts by Roswell Rogers, dated September 23, 1960 and November 21, 1960. Also includes detailed set designs and blueprint ideas.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Working screenplay by Ed James, dated November 21, 1952.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Lobby card; featuring Debi Doyle, no date.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: First final draft by Eugene B. Rodney, dated September 26, 1947.

did.unittitle: PASSING OF THE THIRD FLOOR BACK (THE)

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Written in three acts by Jerome K. Jerome, no date.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: First draft Screenplay by Susan Ertz, no date.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Third draft by A.E. Hotchner, dated 1963.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Revised draft "A" by A.E. Hotchner, dated 1963.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Screenplay by Stanley Roberts, no date.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Unprocessed series. This material is unavailable for access. Please see LSC's Access to Unprocessed Collections Policy for more information about levels of discovery and access of special collections materials and registering interest in unprocessed collections.
